name: Vía Verde de la Plata from Barcial del Barco — circular by the río Esla
name_original: Vía Verde de la Plata desde Barcial del Barco — circular por el río Esla
region_intro: Hiking around Barcial Del Barco offers routes through a landscape shaped by the confluence of the Esla and Órbigo rivers, creating fertile plains and rich ecosystems. The area is situated on a hill overlooking these river systems, which are part of the Natura 2000 network. Many trails follow the historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road and pilgrimage route, often utilizing old railway lines for accessible paths. This region provides a mix of riverine environments and open fields for exploration. Best hiking trails around Barcial Del Barco The most popular hiking route is Vía de la Plata and Vía Verde del Oeste Loop, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines historical sights with scenic river views. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Semillero Lagoons Loop, a moderate 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This circular route explores the El Semillero Lagoons and the Esla River banks, offering serene natural beauty. Local hikers also love the Vía Verde de la Plata from Barcial del Barco — circular by the río Esla, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through riverine areas and cultivated fields, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Barcial Del Barco is defined by riverbanks, historic Vía de la Plata routes, and expansive plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate trails. The routes in Barcial Del Barco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Barcial Del Barco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Barcial Del Barco?
A: Barcial Del Barco offers a variety of hiking trails, primarily characterized by riverine environments, historic routes, and expansive plains. You'll find paths along the Esla and Órbigo rivers, often following sections of the ancient Vía de la Plata and old railway lines. There are over 18 routes available, ranging from easy walks to moderate trails.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Barcial Del Barco has several easy routes. Out of the 18 available hikes, 8 are classified as easy. These trails are generally flat and follow well-defined paths, often along the old railway lines or riverbanks, making them accessible for a relaxed stroll.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, particularly around the Esla and Órbigo river confluence, which forms part of the Natura 2000 network. You can explore the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305470296/el-semillero-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Semillero Lagoons&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy views of the Esla River. Historic bridges are also prominent, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails around Barcial Del Barco are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305470296/el-semillero-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Semillero Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305437921/via-verde-de-la-plata-from-barcial-del-barco-circular-by-the-rio-esla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata from Barcial del Barco — circular by the río Esla&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through riverine areas and cultivated fields.

Q: Is Barcial Del Barco a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, the area offers several family-friendly options, especially the easier routes that follow the old railway lines. These paths are generally flat and well-maintained, providing a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages. The natural landscapes along the rivers also offer opportunities for wildlife spotting.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Barcial Del Barco?
A: Most trails in the Barcial Del Barco region, particularly those along the Vía de la Plata and riverbanks, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry water for them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Barcial Del Barco?
A: The best times for hiking in Barcial Del Barco are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the open plains, while winter can bring cooler temperatures, though hiking is still possible.

Q: Are there any specific regulations or permits required for hiking?
A: No specific hiking permits are generally required for the trails around Barcial Del Barco. The routes are publicly accessible, often following historical paths like the Vía de la Plata or repurposed railway lines. However, always respect private property and natural protected areas.

Q: Where can I find parking for the hiking trails?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Barcial Del Barco, particularly near the old train station which serves as a starting point for sections of the Vía de la Plata. For trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305470296/el-semillero-lagoons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Semillero Lagoons Loop&lt;/a&gt;, you can often find parking in nearby villages or designated areas close to the trailheads.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Barcial Del Barco?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the peaceful riverine landscapes, the historical significance of the Vía de la Plata, and the accessibility of the paths, which often follow old railway lines away from traffic.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: Barcial Del Barco is a small town, but its location within the wider province of Zamora means you can find amenities. While specific cafes or pubs directly on the trails might be limited, you can find options for food and accommodation within Barcial Del Barco itself or in larger nearby towns like Benavente.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The river systems of the Esla and Órbigo, part of the Natura 2000 network, create a rich ecosystem. You might spot various bird species, especially water birds, along the riverbanks and near the lagoons. The area's diverse flora and fauna contribute to a vibrant natural experience.
tips: The 'Vía la Plata' pilgrim route passes by the old and disused train station in the small town of Barcial del Barco, south of Benavente.
Bridge converted from a disused railway line that crosses the Esla River and connects this incredible natural area along the Vía de la Plata Natural Trail.
A beautiful picture when we cross this bridge on the Vía Verde del Oeste or better known as the Vía Verde de la Plata. A luxury for all lovers of hiking, gravel or mountain biking.
